title: mentha arvensis subsp. praecox
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. praecox, also known as Japanese wild mint, is a subspecies of the peppermint plant that is native to East Asia and has naturalized in many parts of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis subsp. praecox include its ability to soothe digestive issues, ease stress and anxiety, and provide relief from headaches and fever. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to make medicinal remedies, flavor food and drinks, and even as a natural insect repellent.
